Title: User logs in successfully
Test Steps:
1. User initiates login request to the application.
2. Application forwards the request to the user management system.
3. User management system checks if the user is logged in.
4. User management system requests Firebase Authentication API to get the current user.
5. Firebase Authentication API returns the current user.
6. User management system confirms the user is logged in and forwards the user object to the application.
7. Application displays the user's profile.
Expected Result: User is successfully logged in and redirected to their profile.

Title: User logs in unsuccessfully due to incorrect password
Test Steps:
1. User initiates login request to the application.
2. Application forwards the request to the user management system.
3. User management system checks if the user is logged in.
4. User management system requests Firebase Authentication API to get the current user.
5. Firebase Authentication API returns null as the user does not exist.
6. User management system confirms the user is not logged in and prompts the user to try again.
7. Application displays an error message.
Expected Result: User is prompted to try logging in again due to incorrect password.

Title: User registers and logs in successfully
Preconditions: User has not registered an account.
Test Steps:
1. User initiates registration and login request to the application.
2. Application forwards the request to the user management system.
3. User management system requests Firebase Authentication API to create a new user.
4. Firebase Authentication API creates a new user.
5. User management system confirms the user is logged in and forwards the user object to the application.
6. Application displays the user's profile.
Expected Result: User is successfully registered and logged in.

Title: Retrieve and display signals
Preconditions: Signals are stored in the database.
Test Steps:
1. Application requests signals from the signal management system.
2. Signal management system forwards the request to the data management system.
3. Data management system queries the database for signals.
4. Database returns the payload containing the signals.
5. Data management system forwards the payload to the signal management system.
6. Signal management system forwards the payload to the application.
7. Application displays the list of signals.
Expected Result: Application displays the list of signals retrieved from the database.

Title: Retrieve and display null signals
Preconditions: No signals are stored in the database.
Test Steps:
1. Application requests signals from the signal management system.
2. Signal management system forwards the request to the data management system.
3. Data management system queries the database for signals.
4. Database returns null as there are no signals.
5. Data management system forwards null to the signal management system.
6. Signal management system forwards null to the application.
7. Application displays null.
Expected Result: Application displays null as there are no signals.

Title: Retrieve train variations
Preconditions: Train variations are stored in the database.
Test Steps:
1. Application requests train variations from the variation management system.
2. Variation management system forwards the request to the data management system.
3. Data management system queries the Trenitalia API for train variations.
4. Trenitalia API returns the payload containing the train variations.
5. Data management system forwards the payload to the variation management system.
6. Variation management system forwards the payload to the application.
7. Application displays the list of train variations.
Expected Result: Application displays the list of train variations retrieved from the Trenitalia API.

Title: Retrieve and display null train variations
Preconditions: No train variations are stored in the database.
Test Steps:
1. Application requests train variations from the variation management system.
2. Variation management system forwards the request to the data management system.
3. Data management system queries the Trenitalia API for train variations.
4. Trenitalia API returns null as there are no train variations.
5. Data management system forwards null to the variation management system.
6. Variation management system forwards null to the application.
7. Application displays null.
Expected Result: Application displays null as there are no train variations.

Title: Set and display calendar
Test Steps:
1. Application sends calendar data to the calendar management system.
2. Calendar management system forwards the calendar data to the data management system.
3. Data management system queries the database for the new calendar data.
4. Database returns the status code indicating success.
5. Data management system forwards the outcome to the calendar management system.
6. Calendar management system forwards the outcome to the application.
7. Application displays the calendar data.
Expected Result: Application displays the calendar data sent by the user.

Test Steps:

2. Calendar management system forwards the request to the data management system.
3. Data management system queries the database for the calendar data.
4. Database returns null as there is no calendar data.
5. Data management system forwards null to the calendar management system.
6. Calendar management system forwards null to the application.
7. Application displays null.
Expected Result: Application displays null as there is no calendar data.

Title: Delete preferred train
Test Steps:
1. Application requests to delete a preferred train.
2. Calendar management system forwards the request to the data management system.
3. Data management system queries the database to delete the train.
4. Database returns the status code indicating success.
5. Data management system forwards the outcome to the calendar management system.
6. Calendar management system forwards the outcome to the application.
7. Application displays the outcome.
Expected Result: Application displays the outcome of the train deletion.

Title: Start notification process
Test Steps:
1. Application requests train variations from the variation management system.
2. Variation management system forwards the request to the data management system.
3. Data management system queries the Trenitalia API for train variations.
4. Trenitalia API returns the payload containing the train variations.
5. Variation management system starts the notification process.
6. Notification system shows notifications to the application.
7. Application displays the notifications.
Expected Result: Application displays the notifications related to train variations.
